gates.evidence: Public page states what the project is, whom it serves, and what concrete work it performs.
A page, workbench, instrument, manuscript packet, or hosted route is reachable from the package.
The project can run a representative action, render a result, or expose a completed manuscript/packet workflow.
Evidence includes a non-sample run, live case, external review, or manuscript readiness proof, not only a template/demo.
Material claims, methods, and source uses are traceable to project files, sources, or reviewer records.
The project has a regression suite, falsifier case, or equivalent challenge condition that prevents easy self-confirmation.
Rendered output is captured on desktop and mobile or the non-web manuscript/software equivalent is documented.
The route, packet, script, or local runtime can be opened and its required artifacts can be fetched or reproduced.
A clearly labeled reviewer, owner, advisor, expert, venue, or Codex technical review note records approval, limits, or remaining defects after inspecting the project result.
The production ledger has no missing blocking gate and no residual evidence slot required for promotion.
